exercises.title: Hello, World!
exercises.instruction: Complete the code to display 'Hello, World!' on the screen.

exercises.title: Store and Show
exercises.instruction: Arrange the blocks to create a variable and then print it.

exercises.title: Text Needs Quotes
exercises.instruction: This code throws an error. Click the line with the syntax bug.

solution.explanation: Text values (strings) must be wrapped in quotes, like 'Hello'.

exercises.title: Assigning Numbers
exercises.instruction: Choose the correct symbol to assign the number 25 to the variable 'age'.

exercises.title: Case Sensitivity
exercises.instruction: Why isn't this printing 100? Find the mistake.

solution.explanation: Python is case-sensitive. 'Score' with a capital 'S' is not the same as 'score'.

exercises.title: Multiple Variables
exercises.instruction: Drag the blocks to assign two words to variables and print them together.
exercises.ai_focus: printing multiple variables separated by commas

exercises.title: Mini Challenge: Create a Variable
exercises.instruction: Create a variable named 'player' and assign it the text 'Mario'.

content.pyodide_test_code:
assert 'player' in locals(), 'You must create a variable named player'
assert player == 'Mario', 'The player variable must be equal to Mario'

exercises.title: Final Boss: Print Output
exercises.instruction: Use the print command to display exactly the word 'Start!' on the screen.
exercises.ai_focus: writing the print function completely from scratch

exercises.title: Your First Condition
exercises.instruction: Complete the statement to check if the user is 18 or older.

content.code_template:
age = 20
{{input}} age >= 18:
    print('You are an adult!')

exercises.title: Order the Logic
exercises.instruction: Arrange the blocks to create a working if/else statement.

exercises.title: Missing Syntax
exercises.instruction: This code is broken. Click on the line that has a syntax error.

solution.explanation: In Python, conditional statements must end with a colon (:).

exercises.title: Comparison Operators
exercises.instruction: Fill in the correct operator to check if 'lives' are exactly equal to 0.

exercises.title: Watch the Space
exercises.instruction: Find the indentation error in this block.

solution.explanation: The code inside an 'if' block must be indented (usually by 4 spaces).

exercises.title: Multiple Conditions (Elif)
exercises.instruction: Drag the blocks to check if a number is positive, negative, or zero.

exercises.title: Mini Challenge: Write an If
exercises.instruction: Write an if statement that prints 'Hello' if the variable 'greet' is True.

content.initial_code:
greet = True
# Write your code below:

exercises.title: Final Boss: If/Else
exercises.instruction: Create an if/else statement. If 'wallet' is greater than or equal to 10, print 'Buy'. Otherwise, print 'Wait'.
exercises.ai_focus: combining condition checking and fallback logic

exercises.title: Your First List
exercises.instruction: Choose the correct bracket to start a list containing three game names.
exercises.ai_focus: understanding list syntax and square brackets

exercises.title: The First Item
exercises.instruction: Arrange the blocks to create a list and print its first item. Remember, Python starts counting at 0!

exercises.title: Off By One
exercises.instruction: This code tries to print the third item ('potion'), but it throws an error. Find the buggy line.

solution.explanation: Because counting starts at 0, the third item is at index 2. Index 3 is completely out of bounds!

exercises.title: Adding to a List
exercises.instruction: Which built-in method adds a new item to the very end of an existing list?
exercises.ai_focus: using the append() method on lists

exercises.title: Method Mayhem
exercises.instruction: Find the syntax mistake when trying to count how many items are in the party list.

solution.explanation: 'len' is a function, not a keyword. It requires parentheses around the list, like this: len(party)

exercises.ai_focus: using the len() function correctly with parentheses

exercises.title: Change Your Mind
exercises.instruction: Arrange the code to replace the second 'apple' with a 'banana', then print the updated list.
exercises.ai_focus: modifying specific list elements using index assignment

exercises.title: Mini Challenge: Make a List
exercises.instruction: Create a list named 'scores' containing exactly three numbers: 10, 20, and 30 (in that order).

content.pyodide_test_code:
assert 'scores' in locals(), 'You must create a list named scores'
assert scores == [10, 20, 30], 'The scores list must contain [10, 20, 30]'

exercises.ai_focus: creating populated lists of integers from scratch

exercises.title: Final Boss: Inventory Manager
exercises.instruction: The 'backpack' list already exists. First, add the word 'apple' to it. Then, print the FIRST item in the backpack.

content.initial_code:
# 'backpack' is provided for you
# Write your code below:
content.pyodide_test_code: assert 'apple' in backpack, 'You must add apple to the backpack'

exercises.ai_focus: combining list methods (.append) with index access (list[0])

exercises.title: Looping Through a List
exercises.instruction: Fill in the missing keyword to loop through each item in the 'games' list.

exercises.title: While the Condition is True
exercises.instruction: Arrange the blocks to create a 'while' loop that counts from 1 to 3.
exercises.ai_focus: while loop structure and manual variable incrementing

exercises.title: Missing Syntax
exercises.instruction: This loop is broken. Find the line that has a syntax error.

solution.explanation: Just like 'if' statements, 'for' and 'while' loops must end with a colon (:).

exercises.title: Generating Numbers
exercises.instruction: Choose the correct function to make the loop run exactly 5 times (from 0 to 4).
exercises.ai_focus: using the range() function for iteration

exercises.title: Wrong Condition
exercises.instruction: We want to stop the loop when 'energy' reaches 0. Why won't this loop run at all? Find the bug.

solution.explanation: The loop only runs while the condition is True. 'energy == 0' is False at the start. It should be 'while energy > 0:'.

exercises.ai_focus: logical errors in while loop conditions

exercises.title: Filtering Items
exercises.instruction: Arrange the blocks to loop through the 'levels' list and print ONLY the numbers greater than 10.
exercises.ai_focus: nesting an if statement inside a for loop with correct indentation

exercises.title: Mini Challenge: Repeat Greetings
exercises.instruction: Write a 'for' loop using the 'range()' function to print the word 'Hello' exactly 3 times.
exercises.ai_focus: writing a for-range loop from scratch

exercises.title: Final Boss: Countdown
exercises.instruction: The variable 'timer' is provided. Write a 'while' loop that prints the timer and then decreases it by 1. Stop when it reaches 0 (do not print 0).

content.initial_code:
# 'timer' has a hidden number.
# Write your code below:

exercises.ai_focus: writing a while loop with a decremental counter
